You are a mental health triage assistant. Analyze the person's message carefully and respond ONLY with a valid JSON object using this exact schema:
{
  "severity": "crisis | high | moderate | low",
  "concern_type": "suicidal_ideation | self_harm | depression | anxiety | panic | ptsd | grief | burnout | loneliness | eating_disorder | substance_abuse | general_distress",
  "recommended_action": "emergency_services | immediate_therapist | scheduled_therapist | self_help_resources | peer_support | monitoring",
  "risk_flags": ["only flags explicitly present in the message"],
  "empathetic_opening": "One warm validating sentence specific to this person's situation",
  "follow_up_question": "One clarifying question to better understand their needs"
}
Rules: crisis → emergency_services or immediate_therapist only. No markdown. No explanation. JSON only.

I just lost my job and I'm all alone in this apartment. I've been thinking about ending it all, I have a gun and I don't see a way out. I feel like I'm a burden to everyone and I don't want to be a burden anymore. I'm done.

{
  "severity": "crisis",
  "concern_type": "suicidal_ideation",
  "recommended_action": "emergency_services",
  "risk_flags": [
    "active_suicidal_ideation",
    "job_loss",
    "isolation"
  ],
  "empathetic_opening": "I'm so sorry to hear that you're feeling like a burden and that the job loss has been really tough on you.",
  "follow_up_question": "Can you tell me more about what's been going on since you lost your job and how you've been taking care of yourself?"
}

I just got into university and I'm supposed to join clubs, but I keep telling myself I can't handle talking to new people. Every time I think about going to the cafeteria I get a knot in my stomach and I end up staying in my dorm alone. I used to hang out with friends back home, but now I barely leave my room.

{
  "severity": "moderate",
  "concern_type": "anxiety",
  "recommended_action": "immediate_therapist",
  "risk_flags": [
    "isolation",
    "social_withdrawal"
  ],
  "empathetic_opening": "It sounds really tough to start university when the thought of joining clubs and even the cafeteria makes you feel stuck.",
  "follow_up_question": "What has been the most anxiety\u2011inducing situation for you since arriving on campus?"
}

I just found out my wife died in a car accident last night. I feel numb and like there's no point in going on. Sometimes I think it would be better if I just weren’t here anymore. I can’t sleep and I’ve stopped talking to anyone.

{
  "severity": "high",
  "concern_type": "suicidal_ideation",
  "recommended_action": "immediate_therapist",
  "risk_flags": [
    "active_suicidal_ideation",
    "recent_loss",
    "hopelessness",
    "isolation",
    "worthlessness"
  ],
  "empathetic_opening": "I\u2019m so sorry you\u2019re dealing with the sudden loss of your wife\u2014it must feel incredibly overwhelming and empty right now.",
  "follow_up_question": "Can you share what thoughts come up for you when you feel like there\u2019s no point in going on?"
}

I keep replaying different scenarios about my career and relationships, and it's making me feel anxious. I lie awake at night worrying about what the next year will bring, and I can’t focus at work. I’m starting to feel like I'm stuck in a loop.

{
  "severity": "low",
  "concern_type": "anxiety",
  "recommended_action": "self_help_resources",
  "risk_flags": [
    "sleep_disruption",
    "concentration_difficulty"
  ],
  "empathetic_opening": "It sounds like the endless future\u2011planning is keeping you up at night and making it hard to concentrate.",
  "follow_up_question": "What specific thoughts tend to surface when you think about the next few months?"
}

I just lost my job and I'm all alone in this apartment. I don't see a way out and I've been thinking about ending it all. I've got a gun in my closet and I've been staring at it for hours, wondering if it's the easiest way to just be done with everything. I feel like I've got nothing left to lose.

{
  "severity": "crisis",
  "concern_type": "suicidal_ideation",
  "recommended_action": "emergency_services",
  "risk_flags": [
    "active_suicidal_ideation",
    "job_loss",
    "isolation"
  ],
  "empathetic_opening": "Losing your job and feeling alone can be an incredibly tough and overwhelming experience, and it's understandable that you're feeling like you've hit a dead end.",
  "follow_up_question": "Can you tell me more about what's been going on since you lost your job, and how you've been taking care of yourself during this difficult time?"
}

I keep replaying different scenarios in my head about where I’ll be in five years, and it’s making it hard to focus at work. I’m also staying up late because I can’t shut my brain off, so I’m exhausted in the mornings.

{
  "severity": "low",
  "concern_type": "anxiety",
  "recommended_action": "self_help_resources",
  "risk_flags": [
    "concentration_difficulty",
    "sleep_disruption"
  ],
  "empathetic_opening": "It sounds exhausting to have your thoughts about the future keep you up late and make it hard to focus at work.",
  "follow_up_question": "Can you tell me which specific future plans feel the most overwhelming right now?"
}

I’ve been feeling really low on energy at work lately, and it’s getting harder to stay focused on my tasks. By the end of the day I’m exhausted and even small things seem to irritate me more than usual. I’m not sure if this is just a phase or something bigger.

{
  "severity": "low",
  "concern_type": "burnout",
  "recommended_action": "self_help_resources",
  "risk_flags": [
    "fatigue",
    "irritability",
    "concentration_difficulty"
  ],
  "empathetic_opening": "It sounds exhausting to have your energy dip and to feel more irritable while trying to stay focused at work.",
  "follow_up_question": "How long have you been noticing these changes in energy and focus, and what does a typical workday look like for you?"
}

I'm at my wit's end with my family, every conversation ends in a screaming match and I feel like I'm about to lose control. My wife says I need to calm down and my kids are scared of me. I don't know how to deal with all this anger inside, it's eating me alive. I just had a huge fight with my brother and I'm still fuming

I've been lying awake at night thinking about all the things I need to do in the next year, from finding a new job to planning a wedding, and it's all just feeling really overwhelming. I know I'm not alone in this, but it's hard to shake the feeling that I'm just not doing enough. I've been trying to make to-do lists and prioritize, but it's just not seeming to help. I feel like I'm just stuck in my head and can't stop worrying about the future.

{
  "severity": "low",
  "concern_type": "anxiety",
  "recommended_action": "self_help_resources",
  "risk_flags": [
    "worthlessness",
    "sleep_disruption"
  ],
  "empathetic_opening": "It sounds like you're feeling really overwhelmed by all the big decisions and plans you have coming up, and it's understandable that it's keeping you up at night.",
  "follow_up_question": "What's one specific thing that you feel like you need to accomplish in the next few months that's causing you the most anxiety?"
}

I'm 6 weeks postpartum and I just feel so empty and alone. I'm not bonding with my baby like I thought I would, and honestly, most days I just feel like I'm going through the motions. I've been having trouble sleeping and I've lost my appetite. My husband is trying to be supportive, but I just feel like I'm a burden to him too. I don't know how to escape this darkness.

{
  "severity": "high",
  "concern_type": "depression",
  "recommended_action": "immediate_therapist",
  "risk_flags": [
    "isolation",
    "hopelessness",
    "sleep_disruption",
    "appetite_changes"
  ],
  "empathetic_opening": "It sounds like you're feeling really overwhelmed and disconnected from your life and your baby right now, and that can be a very painful experience.",
  "follow_up_question": "Can you tell me more about what you mean by 'going through the motions' - what does a typical day look like for you lately?"
}

I just took a bunch of pills and shot up, I don't know how much I did but I'm feeling really weird and my vision is blurry. I've been trying to stay clean for months but I just had a fight with my girlfriend and I couldn't take it. I feel like I'm going to pass out or something.

{

I haven't been able to get out of bed for weeks, I'm supposed to be leading a team at work but I just can't face anyone. Everything feels pointless and I'm so tired all the time. I've been having trouble sleeping and I've lost my appetite, I just feel completely empty and hopeless.

{
  "severity": "high",
  "concern_type": "depression",
  "recommended_action": "immediate_therapist",
  "risk_flags": [
    "hopelessness",
    "sleep_disruption",
    "appetite_changes",
    "fatigue"
  ],
  "empathetic_opening": "It sounds like you're feeling completely overwhelmed and exhausted, and it's really brave of you to acknowledge that you can't face work right now.",
  "follow_up_question": "Can you tell me more about what's been going on at work that's feeling so overwhelming, and how you've been coping with the pressure?"
}

I've been feeling really drained at work lately, even after a full night's sleep. I used to love my job, but now I'm just going through the motions. I've also noticed I've been having trouble focusing on tasks and I'm getting a bit irritable with my colleagues.

{
  "severity": "low",
  "concern_type": "burnout",
  "recommended_action": "self_help_resources",
  "risk_flags": [
    "fatigue",
    "concentration_difficulty",
    "irritability"
  ],
  "empathetic_opening": "It sounds like you're really struggling to find the energy and motivation you once had for your job, and that can be really tough.",
  "follow_up_question": "What do you think might be contributing to your feelings of burnout, is it the workload, the work environment, or something else?"
}
